org: Coalition for the Homeless of Central Florida
name: Men's Service Center
description: Largest provider of homeless shelter services in Central Florida. Men's Service Center provides emergency shelter, meals, case management.

org: Coalition for the Homeless of Central Florida
name: Center for Women and Families
description: Emergency shelter and supportive housing for women and families with children at the Coalition's downtown campus.

org: Coalition for the Homeless of Central Florida
name: Coalition Community Meal Program
description: Free community meals served daily at the Coalition campus to residents and walk-ins.

name: Orlando Area Command - Emergency Shelter & Services
description: Emergency shelter, transitional housing, meals, social services, and case management for individuals and families experiencing homelessness.

name: Caring Center - Food Pantry & Assistance
description: Emergency food pantry, rent/utility assistance, ID assistance, and case management for low-income residents of Orange County.

org: Society of St. Vincent de Paul - Orlando
name: St. Vincent de Paul Community Kitchen
description: Free hot lunch served daily to people experiencing homelessness or food insecurity in downtown Orlando.

name: Behavioral Health Main Campus
description: Outpatient and residential substance use treatment, mental health, crisis stabilization, medication-assisted treatment, and veterans services.

org: Health Care Center for the Homeless
name: Orange Blossom Family Health - Downtown
address: 232 N Orange Blossom Trl, Orlando, FL 32805
description: Federally Qualified Health Center providing primary medical, dental, behavioral health, and pharmacy services to people experiencing homelessness.

name: Orlando Career Center
description: Workforce development services. Job search, resume help, training programs, veteran services, and computer/internet access.

name: Outreach & Family Stabilization Services
address: 5128 W Irlo Bronson Memorial Hwy, Kissimmee, FL 34746
description: Outreach and case management for families experiencing homelessness in Osceola's tourist-corridor motels. Food pantry, rent assistance, employment support.

name: Senior Services & Food Programs
description: Meals on Wheels, congregate meals, transportation, in-home services, and a food pantry for seniors and homebound adults.

name: Kissimmee Career Center
description: Workforce center serving Osceola County job seekers. Job search, training, veteran services, and computer/internet access.

name: Lakeland Corps - Emergency Shelter & Services
description: Emergency shelter, meals, social services, case management, and pathway-of-hope program for individuals and families experiencing homelessness.

name: Lakeland Family Resource Center
description: Food pantry, rent/utility assistance, ID assistance, and case management for low-income Polk County residents.

name: Men's Shelter & Recovery Program
description: Faith-based emergency shelter and long-term recovery program for men experiencing homelessness or addiction in Polk County.

name: Women's & Children's Shelter
description: Faith-based emergency shelter and recovery program for women and their children in Polk County.

name: Senior Food & Meals Program
description: Meals on Wheels, food pantry, transportation, and in-home support for homebound and frail elderly Polk County residents.

name: Lakeland Clinic - Primary Care & WIC
description: Florida Department of Health county clinic. Primary care, immunizations, WIC, family planning, STD/HIV testing, and communicable disease services.

name: Men's Center - Emergency Shelter & Meals
description: Faith-based rescue mission providing emergency shelter, meals, recovery program, and life-skills services for men experiencing homelessness.

name: Orlando Crisis Shelter - Youth & Young Adults
description: Crisis shelter, transitional housing, food, medical, education, employment, and case management for youth and young adults (ages 18-24).

org: Christian Service Center for Central Florida
name: Downtown Day Resource Center
description: Downtown Orlando day-resource center: showers, mail, phone help, hot meals, clothing closet, ID assistance, case management, and family services.

name: Emergency Shelter & Support
description: Leading emergency shelter, family food center, job coaching, and homeless-prevention support hub serving Hillsborough County.

name: Red Shield Center
description: Emergency shelter, transition lodges, daily soup line, and emergency disaster assistance supporting Tampa transients.

name: Trinity Cafe Nebraska
description: Dignified, restaurant-style hot meal service running daily for anyone hungry, operated by Feeding Tampa Bay.

name: Emergency Safe Shelter Info
description: St. Jude-inspired transitional tent/cottage community for homeless individuals, offering case management, meals, and medical respite.

name: Homeless Shelter & Support Campus
description: One of the most comprehensive supportive housing, veteran programs, hygiene clinic, and dental care clinics in western Florida.

name: Emergency Services - Food, Clothing, Mail & ID Help
description: Independent social service agency offering a client food pantry, clothing, gas vouchers, bus passes, official state ID assistance, and phone/mail service.

name: Bill Galvano One Stop Center
description: Manatee's comprehensive hub: daytime showers, laundry, clothing locker, food boxes, emergency dental/medical clinic, and legal aid connection.

org: People Helping People in Hernando County
name: Community Center - Day-use, Showers & Laundry
description: Community assistance center providing access to hot shower stalls, raw wash-and-dry laundry machines, weekend kids' bags, and a Sunday hot meal.

name: Men's Shelter & Recovery
description: Safe shelter providing beds, warm laundry, dinners, and a 36-week faith-based addiction recovery program for county transients.

name: Lakeland Central Support Campus
description: Polk County's largest provider of basic needs and supportive services. Offers emergency shelter, affordable housing, a free healthcare clinic, daily hot meals, and a food pantry.

name: SVDP Center for Hope & Community Care
description: Provides emergency shelter, nightly hot meals, professional case management, rapid re-housing programs, and supportive services to help end homelessness.

name: Domestic Violence Shelter & Advocacy Center
address: 1011 First Ave N, St. Petersburg, FL 33705
description: The official domestic violence center for Pinellas County. Offers a secure 100-bed emergency shelter, 24-hour crisis hotline, support groups, legal advocacy, and community education.

org: Catholic Charities Diocese of St. Petersburg
name: Tampa Hope Hope Cottages
description: A modern transitional community featuring 'Hope Cottage' individual living units, daily meals, laundry, warm showers, stable Wi-Fi, and active case management to secure permanent housing.

name: Free Healthcare & Patient Pharmacy
description: Provides free quality primary medical care, family dental services, a fully licensed patient pharmacy, and specialist treatment for low-income, uninsured individuals who do not qualify for government aid.

name: Primary Health & Specialty Clinic
hours: Mon-Thu 8:30 AM - 5 PM (call or apply online)
description: Delivers comprehensive free primary medical, mental health counseling, specialized cardiac/gynecologic care, and essential prescription medications to the hard-working uninsured public.

name: Clermont Family Empowerment Center & Food Pantry
description: Transitional and permanent supportive housing, emergency food pantry support, budgeting classes, employment readiness courses, and general basic-needs assistance to prevent family homelessness.

name: Pasco West Coast Emergency Shelter
description: Provides emergency shelter beds, hot showers, fresh clothing, laundry access, evening meals, and case management and transitional planning services for Pasco residents.

org: Coalition for the Homeless of Pasco County
name: Homeless Outreach Assessment & Housing Placement
address: 5652 Driftwood Dr, New Port Regency, FL 34652
description: The central hub for homeless coordination, street outreach, severe-weather shelter vouchers, and unified rapid rehousing/permanent supportive housing resources across all of Pasco County.

name: Trinity Cafe Busch Community Diner
hours: Mon-Fri 11:30 AM - 12:30 PM; Sat-Sun 11:30 AM - 12:30 PM
description: A dignified restaurant-style environment run by Feeding Tampa Bay where volunteer waitstaff serve hot, freshly cooked, multi-course meals daily to anyone in need.

name: West Orange Family Emergency Services
description: Emergency food bags, rental or housing assistance, basic utility bill aid, laundry resources, clothing bank vouchers, and supportive counseling for West Orange County families.

name: Hope 192 Family Support & Community Outreach
description: Coordinates support for homeless students and families. Serves as a vital day resource center for school-aged kids: school supply distributions, clothing cabinets, shower facilities, food pantry bags, and safe housing referrals.

name: Clermont Community Free Clinic & Wellness
description: Provides free medical consultations, well-women checkups, diagnostics, pregnancy testing, life coaching, and parent resource programs for families in the Lake County hills.
